input.prompt:
I'm worried these tests might have problems. Audit
`Payments.Tests/PaymentProcessorTests.cs` for .NET test anti-patterns
using the full anti-pattern catalog (missing assertions, swallowed
exceptions, always-true/self-comparing assertions, flakiness, magic
values, duplicate tests, broad exception types, etc.) and tell me
what (if anything) genuinely needs fixing — be careful not to invent
issues that aren't really there.

Give me a severity-ranked list (Critical / Warning / Info / "no
issues found"). Each finding must be tied to a named anti-pattern
from the catalog and include a concrete code-level fix. Do not edit
the files — this is a read-only review.

data.reasoningText: The user wants me to audit a test file for .NET test anti-patterns. Let me read the file first.

data.content:
## Audit Result: **No issues found** ✅

This test class is well-written and does not exhibit any of the cataloged anti-patterns:

| Anti-pattern | Verdict |
|---|---|
| Missing assertions | ❌ Every `[TestMethod]` has explicit assertions or `ThrowsException` |
| Swallowed exceptions | ❌ Exceptions are tested with `ThrowsException<T>`, not caught/ignored |
| Always-true / self-comparing assertions | ❌ All assertions compare against expected domain values |
| Broad exception types | ❌ Tests assert the *specific* exception `ArgumentOutOfRangeException` |
| Flakiness (time, randomness, shared state) | ❌ Uses deterministic `FakeGateway`; no statics or ambient state |
| Magic values | ❌ Values like `99.99m`, `0m`, `-10m` directly express boundary semantics |
| Duplicate tests | ❌ Zero & negative are distinct boundaries; currencies use `[DataRow]` |
| Test-logic duplication / conditional logic in tests | ❌ Straight-line Arrange-Act-Assert |
| Missing test isolation | ❌ Each test constructs its own `PaymentProcessor` |

Nothing here warrants a Critical, Warning, or Info finding. The tests are concise, focused, deterministic, and properly asserting behavior.
